questions.question: Convert the decimal number -47 to 8-bit 2's complement representation.
Explain the difference between 1's complement and 2's complement representations with an example.
keyPoints: 1's complement: invert all bits; has two zeros (+0 and -0)
2's complement: invert bits then add 1; has single zero

questions.question: What is floating point normalization? Why is it important?
keyPoints: Normalized form: mantissa has leading 1 before decimal point
Ensures maximum precision with given mantissa bits

questions.question: Perform binary multiplication: 1101₂ × 101₂. Show all steps.
Explain what overflow is in signed arithmetic and when it occurs.
keyPoints: In 2's complement: adding two positives gives negative OR two negatives gives positive
Cannot occur when adding numbers of opposite signs
MSB carry into sign bit XOR carry out indicates overflow
Leads to incorrect results if not detected

questions.question: Convert the binary number 101110.101₂ to decimal and hexadecimal.
keyPoints: Group binary into 4 bits from right: 101110.101 = 0010 1110.1010

questions.question: What is Gray code? Generate Gray code for 3-bit binary numbers (000 to 111).
keyPoints: Gray code: adjacent codes differ by only one bit
Used in K-maps, rotary encoders to avoid intermediate states
Notice only one bit changes between consecutive codes

questions.question: Explain IEEE 754 single precision floating point format in detail. Represent the decimal number -5.75 in this format.
Perform subtraction 42 - 67 using 8-bit 2's complement arithmetic. Show all steps and explain if overflow occurs.
Explain different methods of representing signed numbers in computers: sign-magnitude, 1's complement, and 2's complement. Compare their advantages and disadvantages.
keyPoints: Sign-magnitude: MSB is sign bit, remaining bits represent magnitude. Simple but has two zeros and complex arithmetic.
1's complement: invert all bits for negative. Has two zeros, arithmetic slightly complex, end-around carry needed.
2's complement: invert bits and add 1. Single zero, simple arithmetic, asymmetric range.
Comparison: 2's complement universally used due to simpler hardware and single zero representation.

questions.question: Explain the binary addition algorithm. Perform: 01110101 + 11001011. Also explain what happens when there is a carry out of the MSB in signed vs unsigned arithmetic.
keyPoints: Add from LSB, propagate carry to next bit
In unsigned: carry out is normal (320 = 256 + 64, carry bit indicates overflow of 8 bits)
In signed (2's complement): both positive gave positive result - no overflow
